mechanism_narrative: A sustained low level of the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, consumer spending growth collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing equity markets to price in prolonged recessionary conditions and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal a synchronized recovery in consumer and capital demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding factory output and tightening capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month growth fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during early expansion phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215.0, or the threshold effect disappears in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates deteriorating labor market quality and hidden slack, which suppresses aggregate demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, which translates into immediate positive repricing of equity assets and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of quarters, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production contracts while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of periods where INDPRO declines alongside CPIAUCSL increases, or the joint signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in walk-forward validation.
